meta.setting: A dusty wagon yard near a Shasta road junction
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in Native community labor and road pressure

research_grounding.title: Redding Rancheria
research_grounding.use: Pit River, Wintu, and Yana people, living sovereignty, traditional homelands, and community identity.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional road, settlement, mining, and work pressure.

image_prompt.subject: Pit River, Wintu, and Yana-connected hands working around a wagon yard: harness repair, freight sorting, and quiet conversation under pine shade
image_prompt.scene: busy 1899 yard near a road junction; dusty wagons, mixed community labor, a law office in the far background, harness leather spread over a barrel, and a rider waiting without being the center
image_prompt.composition: square layered work scene with hands and harness in the foreground, freight and horses in the middle, and social tension behind
image_prompt.camera: waist-level game cutscene angle, intimate but respectful, with practical work objects closest to the viewer
material_cues: freight rope frayed at the knot
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the work ordinary enough to be overlooked and important enough to hold the whole road together.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine work-yard scene
render_traits: slightly simplified but respectful human and horse silhouettes
modest texture resolution on leather, rope, canvas, dust, and pine
hard ambient occlusion under wagon beds, barrels, hands, and tack
hot afternoon light broken by pine shade
believable 1899 wagon-yard clothing, tools, and freight
style_modifiers.palette: dust tan, oiled leather brown, pine green, canvas gray, iron black, faded work-cloth blue, and hot road ochre.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, crate labels, paperwork, or invented Native text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The people, harness work, freight, wagons, and road-yard setting are fully readable.
Native presence is ordinary, respectful, skilled, and grounded in work.
No invented sacred detail, fake language, costume spectacle, or modern object appears.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
